data.narrative: Student demonstrated exceptional depth in requirement extraction, moving beyond prose to identify a systemic financial liability conflict. They correctly identified snapshotting as a technical mitigation for policy changes, showing high-level architectural foresight.

dimension_scores.observation: Extracted 17 distinct requirements and identified a multi-stakeholder financial contradiction between paragraphs.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student identified the Financial Liability Conflict where the platform makes promises using provider money.

dimension_scores.observation: Recognized that cancellation logic touches almost every other platform pillar (earnings, payouts, disputes).
dimension_scores.evidence: Student listed touched requirements including P2, P5, A1, and A2.

dimension_scores.observation: Identified timezone handling as a silent failure mode and track everything as a scope creep risk.
dimension_scores.evidence: Student flagged timezone handling as something that will silently break bookings in a multi-city marketplace.

dimension_scores.observation: Proposed two mutually exclusive, implementable policy options for the PM.
dimension_scores.evidence: Option 1: Platform-Owned Fixed Policy vs Option 2: Provider-Defined Policy with Platform Floor.

dimension_scores.observation: Seamlessly integrated a mid-module PM clarification by immediately flagging the blast radius.
dimension_scores.evidence: Immediately identified the contradiction between Paragraph 1 and Paragraph 2 upon receiving the update.

dimension_scores.observation: Caught that individual correctness (provider autonomy) doesn't guarantee system correctness (universal refund).
dimension_scores.evidence: Identified that the platform was making a financial promise without defining who absorbs the cost.

strengths: Detecting The Silence Pass (Timezones, Refund liability) before they were mentioned.
Proposing concrete solutions rather than just flagging problems.
data.recommendation: In Module 02, apply this same blast radius thinking to the dependency graph to find the critical path.

public_evidence.student_action: Extracted 17 requirements and identified a critical refund contradiction.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Proves ability to find unstated assumptions and financial contradictions in product specs.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Ratio of 7:10 explicit to implicit requirements.
